title: Comarobatia lasiococca
intro: Comarobatia lasiococca, also known as the "hairy leaf comarobatia," is a shrub-like plant species native to the western United States. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its use in traditional medicine to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Comarobatia lasiococca include iridoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments in herbal medicine.

Comarobatia lasiococca is a shrub or small tree that produces small, yellow flowers in the spring.
